resources.string: No browser available to open Real-Debrid.
Couldnt reach Real-Debrid. Check your internet connection or try a different network.t reach Real-Debrid. Check your internet connection or try a different network.

Secure connection to Real-Debrid failed. Your network appears to be intercepting or downgrading HTTPS traffic to api.real-debrid.com. Disable captive portals, VPNs, secure web gateways, or TLS inspection, or try a different network.
The device authorization session expired. Start again.
Notifications remain disabled. Open system settings if you want reminder alerts.
1. Tap Connect Real-Debrid to request a device code.
2. Copy the code if you want, then approve the device on Real-Debrid.
3. If your browser handoff is interrupted, use Open Authorization Page below.
4. Return here while DebridHub polls for completion.
You can open Trust Center first if you want to inspect privacy, security, diagnostics, and compliance details.
DebridHub talks directly to the official Real-Debrid API. Tokens stay on device and no backend is involved.
Track your Real-Debrid subscription and renew before it lapses.
Notifications are already enabled on this device.
Notifications are currently disabled for DebridHub. You can try the permission prompt again or re-enable alerts from system settings.
You can enable notifications now or later. Reminder alerts start once your account is connected.
Waiting for Real-Debrid approval. After you approve the device, come back here and DebridHub will finish the login automatically.
DebridHub will schedule these local notifications on this device:
No future reminders are planned right now. This can happen if the expiry date is very close, already passed, or your selected reminder windows are already in the past.

plurals.name: reminders_notification_expires_in
plurals.item: Your Real-Debrid subscription expires in %1$s day
Your Real-Debrid subscription expires in %1$s days

resources.string: Reminders are planned, but notifications are currently disabled for DebridHub. Re-enable them in system settings if you want these alerts to fire.
Refresh your account to preview local reminders.
Current scope is intentionally narrow: account auth, account-status reads, local reminder scheduling, and local diagnostics export.
The app does not currently implement unrestrict, downloads, torrent management, streaming, generated-link sharing, or multi-user account management.

DebridHub uses official Real-Debrid API endpoints and the documented device-auth flow. Users are still responsible for following Real-Debrids own Terms and account rules.s own Terms and account rules.
Diagnostics export currently includes app version, OS version, last sync timestamp, account expiry state, and limited non-sensitive runtime flags.
Diagnostics export excludes access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, username, email, and full account history.
This is in-app product copy based on the current implementation, not a marketing layer or webview.
See exactly how DebridHub handles auth, storage, diagnostics, and feature boundaries.
Diagnostics preview is not available yet.
DebridHub has no backend. The app talks directly to Real-Debrid from your device.
There is no analytics SDK, tracking pixel, crash-reporting service, or remote account sync in the current app.
Reminder notifications are local to this device and are scheduled using your locally stored account status.
Diagnostics export is manual. Nothing is sent anywhere unless you explicitly share the exported file yourself.
Authentication uses Real-Debrids official OAuth2 device flow rather than password collection.s official OAuth2 device flow rather than password collection.
Android stores auth state with EncryptedSharedPreferences.
iOS stores auth state in Keychain and migrates legacy NSUserDefaults auth state on first read.
Disconnect clears saved auth state and cancels reminders. DebridHub does not need a companion server to function.
